old_claim.note: Modern Stock Exchange provides a capability that enables this technology without being the only possible path.

new_claim_absent: The Singer installment contract operated through retail delivery and scheduled payments; the source does not make stock-exchange trading part of the method.

source_shape.source_locator: The Harvard exhibit says mechanization and mass-produced consumer durables made installment plans broadly available in the nineteenth century and quotes the Singer company plan in 1856.
source_shape.source_claim_summary: Harvard documents installment sales through durable-goods firms, retail agents, down payments, and scheduled customer payments.
source_shape.source_support_rationale: Corporate finance may support firms generally, but it is not the transactional mechanism by which the source says consumers acquired goods on installments.

ontology_before: consumer_installment_credit inherited directly from stock_exchange_modern as an origin or component dependency.
ontology_after: consumer_installment_credit no longer inherits from stock_exchange_modern; the source-pinned scope is represented by its remaining prerequisites.
invariant_preserved_or_changed: Changed: consumer_installment_credit no longer has a direct prerequisite edge from stock_exchange_modern.
would_reject_if: Direct historical evidence showed that stock_exchange_modern was an operational prerequisite for the source-pinned consumer_installment_credit milestone rather than later context or a parallel practice.
short_reason: A securities exchange is not part of the documented retail installment contract.
